method_reason: Used because this is an ad-level outcome and each participant can contribute multiple ad rows. A normal pairwise t-test would incorrectly assume all ad observations are independent. Clustered standard errors by student_id account for within-participant dependence.
family_reason: This is a simple-effect comparison for the Device × Discount interaction. It tests whether discount types differ within one specific device group.
multiple_comparison_reason: Holm correction is used because multiple pairwise comparisons are conducted within the same family of tests. The Holm-adjusted p-value controls the family-wise error rate and should be used for the main pairwise interpretation.
result_interpretation_sentence: 50OFF had a higher mean/proportion than NONE for purchase_intention by 2.012. The difference remains statistically significant after Holm correction (raw p < .001, Holm p < .001).
reporting_recommendation: Report as a significant follow-up pairwise effect using the Holm-adjusted p-value.
